channel.title: LIBRCHAIN 区块链新闻雷达
channel.description: 每小时自动更新的区块链新闻、博客与 X 热点聚合

item.title: Calls Mount for UK to Ban Political Donations Made in Crypto
item.description: A former Labour minister has argued that foreign “enemies of democracy” are using crypto political donations to undermine British politics.

item.title: Bitcoin nears $70K as PMI boost sees BTC price leave Iran woes behind
item.description: Bitcoin price strength received a surprise bullish catalyst from US manufacturing data, helping to relieve tensions over Iran as US stocks floundered.

item.title: Crypto world faces growing pressure to relent on stablecoin rewards to win bigger prize
item.description: As much as crypto negotiators paint bank lobbyists' campaign against stablecoin yield as unfair, it landed a blow that one dramatic move could end.

item.title: ProCap boosts Bitcoin holdings to 5,457 BTC, aims to narrow NAV discount
item.description: The Bitcoin treasury company repurchased 782,000 shares below NAV as it increases Bitcoin exposure and works to close the gap between market price and asset value.

item.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum ETFs Snap Five-Week Losing Streak as Crypto Funds Add $1 Billion
item.description: Bitcoin ETFs rebounded as crypto funds saw $1 billion in fresh inflows last week—but is the worst really over?

item.title: Nasdaq follows Cboe joining world of 'binary bets' as prediction market craze hits Wall Street
item.description: The exchange has filed a proposal with the SEC to list yes-or-no bets on the Nasdaq-100 amid continued demand for prediction markets.

item.title: XRP faces $650M sell risk as charts hint at prices below $1
item.description: XRP’s weakening technical setup suggests a drop below $1 could be in the cards over the next few weeks as supply rises on exchanges.

item.title: Ethereum Price, BitMine Shares Jump as Tom Lee's Treasury Reports Latest Buy
item.description: Publicly traded Ethereum treasury BitMine Immersion Technologies added to its ETH stack last week despite its recent decline.

item.title: Bitcoin surges above $68,000 amid muted stock market reaction to Iran war
item.description: At their worst levels, U.S. stock index futures had been down more than 2%, but equity markets are barely lower one hour into Monday's trading session.

item.title: Strategy Spends $200 Million on Bitcoin, Raises STRC Dividend Yet Again
item.description: Strategy disclosed its third-largest Bitcoin purchase of the year, scooping up the asset using proceeds that partially came from STRC.

item.title: Tom Lee's Bitmine boosts ether holdings to 4.47 million tokens with $98 million ETH purchase
item.description: The ether treasury firm now has nearly $10 billion in assets and more than $6 billion of ETH staked.

item.title: Anthropic Claude Outage: AI Chatbot Goes Offline Following Trump, Pentagon Blowup
item.description: Just days after President Trump slammed Anthropic for disputes over wartime use of its AI, a major Claude outage has stretched on for hours.

item.title: EU banks’ euro-pegged stablecoin in talks with crypto exchanges to ensure liquidity
item.description: Qivalis is a group of 12 major EU banks developing a euro-pegged stablecoin they plan to debut in the second half of the year.

item.title: Nexo is back in the US: What changed after the 2023 crypto lending crackdown?
item.description: After the 2023 crackdown, Nexo reenters the US with a partner-led model. What is different, and what should users watch?

item.title: CoinDesk 20 performance update: NEAR Protocol (NEAR) jumps 12.4% over weekend
item.description: Solana (SOL), up 2.1% from Friday, was also among the top performers.

item.title: Iranian crypto outflows jump 700% minutes after U.S.-Israeli airstrikes, Elliptic says
item.description: The blockchain analytics firm said flows from Iran’s largest exchange spiked immediately after U.S.-Israeli strikes on Tehran, pointing to possible capital flight.

item.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
item.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

item.title: Michael Saylor’s Strategy buys $204M of Bitcoin in 101st purchase
item.description: Strategy added 3,015 Bitcoin at $67,700 a piece in its 101st purchase, marking another buy below cost basis and lifting total holdings to 720,737 BTC.

item.title: Energym AI dystopia goes viral as crypto projects tout user-owned AI agents
item.description: A Black Mirror‑style “Energym” spoof imagining 80% of jobs lost to AI is circulating as tech companies begin slashing thousands of roles, and white-collar job openings hit decade lows.

item.title: White hat helps recover $1.8M after $2.3M Foom Cash exploit
item.description: Foom Cash lost $2.26 million in an exploit tied to a Groth16 verifier misconfiguration, but a white hat recovered $1.84 million of the funds.

item.title: Turkey's ruling party unveils 10% crypto income tax proposal
item.description: The bill proposes a 10% tax on gains from regulated crypto platforms, withheld quarterly, with the president having the power to adjust the rate between 0% and 20%.

item.title: Strategy purchased more than $200 million in bitcoin last week
item.description: The latest purchase, funded through common and preferred stock sales, lifted total holdings to 720,737 coins valued at more than $47 billion.

item.title: Morning Minute: Bitcoin Crashes, Rebounds as Iran War Begins
item.description: Hyperliquid that was the weekend's crypto winner, setting a new record and being featured by a major news outlet's weekend market coverage.

item.title: Will Bitcoin crash if oil prices hit $100 per barrel?
item.description: Oil prices are rising amid the US-Iran conflict, but whether Bitcoin will suffer is up for debate, as history suggests a bullish BTC price outlook.

item.title: South Korea investigates seed phrase leak in photo leading to $4.8 million crypto theft from tax authority
item.description: The National Tax Service said it had intended to provide a vivid shot of the seizure by sharing a photo.

item.title: Anthony Pompliano’s ProCap Financial buys 450 bitcoin, steps up share buybacks
item.description: The acquisition makes ProCap the 19th largest publicly traded holder of bitcoin, while lowering the company’s average cost basis per coin.

item.title: Strategy has acquired 3,015 BTC for ~$204.1 million at ~$67,700 per bitcoin. As of 3/1/2026, we hodl 720,737 $BTC acquired for ~$54.77 billion at ~$75,985 per bitcoin. $MSTR $STRC - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiW0FVX3lxTE1DYzNXLTIzdVJzQXNwaTZSbmVOODNSZVNsSFV6b2NiR05seVZINkJHck5RX1hOaHF6X05HV3FHd1k5LXZldW9VUk1nR1huRVY0cEtMRUdzRXVvME0?oc=5" target="_blank">Strategy has acquired 3,015 BTC for ~$204.1…

item.title: Altcoins Outperform Bitcoin With Double-Digit Weekly Gains
item.description: Polkadot, Near, and Jupiter jumped as Bitcoin stabilized, but experts warn the move is a positioning rebound, not a trend shift.

item.title: Crypto funds rebound with $1B inflows after five-week slump
item.description: Crypto ETPs saw $1 billion in inflows last week, led by $787 million into US spot Bitcoin ETFs, ending a five-week outflow streak of nearly $4 billion

item.title: Sony Bank and JPYC to test instant yen stablecoin buys from customer accounts
item.description: Sony Bank and JPYC will study real-time transfers enabling customers to buy the yen-pegged stablecoin JPYC directly from their bank accounts.

item.title: Battered bitcoin could find solace in war-led 'debasement' trade
item.description: Your day-ahead look for March 2, 2026

item.title: Hong Kong, Shanghai authorities to test blockchain for cargo trade data
item.description: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority and Shanghai partners will study a blockchain cross-border platform linking cargo trade data, e-bills of lading and finance under Project Ensemble.

item.title: Bitcoin outperforms equities in risk-off session as Iran conflict enters third day
item.description: Bitcoin rebounded to $66,500 after weekend strikes on Iran triggered $300 million in liquidations. Oil jumped, equities slid and select DeFi tokens outperformed.

item.title: U.S. equity futures fall in pre-market trading as oil, gold retreat from highs
item.description: Oil and gold pulled back after surging on the breakout of hostilities in Iran, while equities and crypto stocks face pressure.

item.title: ‘This is not World War III:’ Five things to know in Bitcoin this week
item.description: Bitcoin avoided an Iran sell-off to start March, but traders still expected BTC price support to give way in bearish market conditions.

item.title: 🇿🇦 LATEST: South African Revenue Service activates CARF, bringing crypto and offshore accounts under global transparency rules. - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiZEFVX3lxTE1zUmg3R0Jrd1B4WFItMUNEcHZybjBBc1d3WTVaOTdhNXpXZEdVdWwyclNIaC1EWnZVQVN1ZWt5OHBWY2dBdUhvUWJYSE9fRGl1V1BZUzFfVVQtbDJhT1FMdTBkZ24?oc=5" target="_blank">🇿🇦 LATEST: South African Reve…

item.title: Hong Kong links up with Shanghai trade authorities to put cargo data on blockchain
item.description: HKMA teams up with mainland regulators to develop a cross-border platform linking cargo data and electronic bills of lading, aiming to cut trade finance friction and plug Chinese supply chains into global markets

item.title: European banks seek exchange partners ahead of 2026 stablecoin launch
item.description: Qivalis, a European banking consortium, is reportedly in talks with crypto exchanges ahead of a planned euro stablecoin launch in the second half of 2026.

item.title: Riot Platforms, Core Scientific earnings, U.S. jobs report: Crypto Week Ahead
item.description: Your look at what's coming in the week starting March 2.

item.title: Aave proposal clears first hurdle with 52.6% support amid governance split
item.description: Tokenholders backed a $42.5 million stablecoin package and 75,000 AAVE allocation for Aave Labs under a proposed DAO-funded revenue model.

item.title: South Korea orders cross-agency probe after repeated crypto custody failures
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister Koo Yun-cheol ordered an inter-agency review of seized crypto wallets after the National Tax Service exposed a seed phrase in a press release.

item.title: Bitcoin slips below $66,000, U.S. stock futures bleed as Iran hits Saudi oil refinery
item.description: Iran has reportedly stepped up attacks against U.S. assets in the middle east.

item.title: Bitcoin to see tailwinds if AI prompts ‘easier monetary policy’: NYDIG
item.description: NYDIG’s Greg Cipolaro says AI could be a “general-purpose technology,” and its effects on the economy could be a boon for Bitcoin.

item.title: Anthropic's AI Used in Iran Strikes After Trump Moved to Cut Ties: WSJ
item.description: Claude was reportedly embedded in U.S. Central Command even as the White House ordered federal agencies to cut all ties with the company.

item.title: Over $9 billion flees bitcoin and ether ETFs in four months
item.description: Record outflows indicate that institutional appetite for digital assets has collapsed.

item.title: Fed could print money to support US conflict with Iran, says Hayes
item.description: Arthur Hayes says the longer Donald Trump engages in expensive “Iranian nation-building,” the more likely it is that the Federal Reserve will boost money supply.

item.title: Finance Minister Pledges Reform After Crypto Handling Failure in Korea
item.description: The pledge follows examples of custody lapses that have exposed weaknesses in how South Korean authorities secure seized crypto.

item.title: Bitcoin, cryptos under pressure as oil spikes 6% and global markets price in U.S.-Iran conflict
item.description: Bitcoin fell back to $66,700 as traditional markets opened to their first chance to price the weekend's military escalation, with oil surging to $77 and Asian equities dropping 1.4%.

item.title: ALERT! Our system detected a suspicious transaction targeting @InverseFinance's contract on Ethereum a few hours ago, resulting in ~$240K in losses. The incident appears to involve a DOLA price manipulation that forced multiple users into liquidation. We - x.com

item.title: Hyperliquid's HYPE token jumps 5% as Iran war brings windfall revenue, JUP gains on supply freeze
item.description: Traders lean into supply compression stories in altcoins as Hyperliquid ramps up token burns and Jupiter freezes new emissions, even as bitcoin churns between $60,000 and $69,000 with muted flow.

item.title: February crypto losses hit lowest level since March 2025, says PeckShield
item.description: PeckShield said fewer mega-hacks, heightened volatility and tighter risk controls may have helped push crypto losses to their lowest level in 11 months.

item.title: Magic Eden winds down EVM, Bitcoin NFT markets to focus on gambling
item.description: Magic Eden is shifting resources from NFTs to its casino platform Dicey, after a closed beta saw $15 million wagered by around 200 users in two months.

item.title: Starting March 1, ALL hourly employees earn a Bitcoin bonus of 21 cents per hour! 🧡 We also offer $1k contribution to Trump Accounts, supporting our employees ' children. 🇺🇸 We aim to be a Maximum-Wage Employer in our respective communities. We look - x.com

item.title: Hyperliquid's Token Rises as Weekend Iran Shock Finds Few Open Markets
item.description: Hyperliquid's token is trading higher on the day as traders use the always-on venue to speculate on ongoing tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Newl interim Supreme Leader Ayatollah Arafi has reportedly been killed in an airstrike. He was appointed just today. - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiYkFVX3lxTE5WWUNNRkNhTXY1cGQ1RnhsZEJyaDlzMy1hOTRESzE3QWU2QkVweXh4VkdBOUUxYl9Hck5yMkRwdDJRMFl0T1IzdHczWmZQdDMtVU5zNEFhZWJwUzN1RVNMMlBn?oc=5" target="_blank">🇮🇷 Newl interim Supreme Leader A…

item.title: 🚨 BREAKING: Crypto social media is discussing the prospect of World War 3 at the highest level since June, 2025. To summarize, based on what has been reported by news outlets: 📌 June 13, 2025 – June 24, 2025: Israel launched strikes on Iranian nuclear - x.com

item.title: What the Iran Conflict Means for Bitcoin's Price
item.description: Attention has shifted to whether the Iran conflict remains contained, as Bitcoin traders weigh elevated oil prices and a rising gold price.

item.title: X removed “financial products” and “crypto” from the list of prohibited industries under its Paid Partnerships Policy. Nikita said the change was an error and has since been corrected, adding that he loves prediction markets. This means that cryptocurrency-relat - x.com

item.title: The Best AI Tools That Actually Respect Your Privacy
item.description: Big Tech AI tools treat your data like a buffet. Here are nine alternatives that don't—and which one wins for your specific threat model.
